purpose: Machine-readable external-reference backlog for broad E5 RF port/S-parameter validation. This is separate from the release slow-shard manifest: it tracks which port families still need external solver/reference evidence, preferably as independent VESSL jobs.
completion_rule: Broad all-port E5 requires every required family to have current_status=broad_e5_passed, required_scope=broad_e5, existing reference/YAML paths present, at least one passed E4/E4-enabling external S-parameter comparison artifact, at least one passed broad E4 external S-parameter comparison artifact that is not merely E4-enabling/narrow, and at least one passed broad-E5 mesh/frequency/geometry envelope artifact. Additionally (T2.2), each family must declare a named AD-vs-FD test (ad_fd_test) that exists, is collected, and is NOT xfail/skip-marked — the differentiability moat is wired into the broad_e5_passed verdict (framework audit #6); families whose extractor is numpy/not-traceable (e.g. coaxial) cannot satisfy this and must resolve it before broad_e5_passed.

requirements.recommended_reference: openEMS or equivalent full-wave two-port lumped/cavity reference with matched/open/short/load cases
missing_evidence: broad external full-wave calibrated lumped-port S11/S21 reference over matched/open/short/load cases
mesh/frequency/geometry envelope against the external reference
requirements.notes: M13/M14 provide analytic extractor and internal replay/passivity evidence. M33 adds one narrow rfx/openEMS two-port PEC-box magnitude comparison, and M47 adds a three-case rfx/openEMS PEC-box position sweep. M56 reran those three M47 PEC-box cases as independent VESSL jobs and aggregated their passed case artifacts; this validates the slow-sim parallel path but does not broaden the physics envelope beyond narrow PEC-box E4-enabling evidence. Broad calibrated-port E5 still requires matched/open/short/load external fixtures and a larger mesh/frequency/geometry envelope.
requirements.ad_fd_test_note: grad-flow tests exist (test_s11_at_freq, test_minimize_s11_at_freq_physical) but no committed AD-vs-FD CONVERGENCE test yet; required before broad_e5_passed.

requirements.recommended_reference: openEMS/HFSS/Meep probe-fed patch or two-probe reference with absolute S11/S21 calibration
missing_evidence: nonuniform/shadow path promotion evidence if public claims include it
requirements.notes: M12/M15 replay the current midpoint-cell convention. M32 adds a narrow probe-fed patch OpenEMS S11 magnitude comparison, but broad absolute wire-port calibration remains blocked.
requirements.ad_fd_test_note: grad-flow smoke only (test_wire_port_sparams_forward::test_forward_wire_port_grad_flows); no AD-vs-FD convergence test yet.

requirements.notes: Existing openEMS evidence supports the narrow laplace/quasi-TEM lane only.
requirements.ad_fd_test_note: AD-vs-FD converged on the MSL S-matrix; @pytest.mark.gpu (runs on the gpu suite, not CPU CI).

requirements.recommended_reference: MEEP/OpenEMS/Palace branch, multimode, and nonuniform waveguide reference set
requirements.notes: Existing M4 evidence is E5-narrow for the rectangular-guide envelope with external MEEP/OpenEMS/Palace references. 2026-05-26 multi-band flux extension: five broad-E5 envelopes (WR-28 Ka, WR-62 Ku, WR-15 V, WR-340 S, WR-10 W) pass vs analytic Airy using compute_waveguide_s_matrix(normalize='flux') — power-flux extraction (extract_waveguide_s_matrix_flux) that resolves both the normalize=False noise floor (~0.06) and the normalize=True path-length-mismatch unitarity violation. Per-band max |S11| diff: WR-28 0.025, WR-62 0.011, WR-15 0.041, WR-340 0.008, WR-10 0.010 (all 4/4 cases, unitarity within +/-0.05 %p). Recipe in docs/guides/sparameter_support_matrix.md; trail in docs/research_notes/20260526_waveguide_flux_silver_bullet.md. Computed on VESSL gpu-rtx4090 (run 369367239898 + WR-28 L=1mm rerun 369367239918). 2026-05-27 multi-mode extension: over-moded (a=22.86 b=4mm, TE10+TE20, 15.5-18.5 GHz) centered-slab broad-E5 envelope passes vs per-mode analytic Airy (4/4 cases, dx 200/250um x eps_r 2/4): per-mode TE10/TE20 |S11| max diff 0.042, |S21| 0.014, AND cross-mode (TE10<->TE20) coupling exactly 0.0000. Required two framework additions: extract_multimode_s_matrix_flux (a40cb1b, power-flux multimode S-matrix; normalize=True multimode was broken with cross-mode 0/0 blow-up to |S|=4.7) and a symmetric E/H co-location stencil (8fcf724) that removes the Yee half-cell-stagger cross-mode leak (cross-overlap O(dx) 0.029 -> exactly 0; single-mode |S11| unchanged 0.025->0.0245). Trail  2026-06-02 T-junction multiport VALIDATED + artifacts gate-wired: far-port + 48mm CPML + converged mesh (dx 1.0/0.667mm) -> full 5.0-7.0 GHz broad-E5 PASS (passivity 1.006, recip 0.001, mesh-conv 0.030, cross-FDTD vs self-converged MEEP res=4000 0.084). MEEP edge self-drift HALVES 0.099->0.042->0.020 over res 500/1000/2000/4000 = converged; rfx strictly mesh-settled (per-freq <0.02) to ~6.2 GHz, 6.4-7.0 GHz within the 0.08 mesh-conv gate but still settling toward the 7.5 GHz TE20 cutoff (peak 0.030 @7.0), converged rfx-vs-MEEP cross-code diff at 7.0 ~0.02-0.04 (under 0.11 tol); full-band cross-FDTD max 0.084 is at the 5.0 GHz CPML-limited low edge where both codes are converged.. Long per-freq-reflection saga root cause = under-resourced CPML (nc=10 vs recipe>=20; physical thickness must be >~1 lambda_g, scales 1/dx) + coarse mesh at TE20-onset edge; NOT a solver/extraction defect (corner-staircase/TE20/intrinsic-floor/SOL hypotheses all falsified). 2026-06-02: superseded dangling refs M4(2026-05-09 narrow envelope)/M30(WR90 generic comparison)/M69(WR90 broad envelope) PRUNED -- they were gitignored/absent in-checkout and are superseded by the present flux-era broad-E5 set (WR-28/62/15/340/10 + multimode + WR90-NU, all passed) plus the T-junction broad-E4 MEEP comparison. current_status flipped to broad_e5_passed; the authoritative gate (check_port_external_references.py) returns this family PASSED on the present passing evidence. Tolerances unchanged (no loosening). 2026-06-11 manifest hygiene (W5.6): pruned 2 dangling YAML refs (vessl_waveguide_band_broad_e5_flux.yaml, vessl_wr90_nu_flux_broad_e5.yaml) — never tracked in-repo (VESSL-side launch files); the runs they launched are evidenced by the listed broad_e5_envelope_artifacts + VESSL run ids 369367239898/369367239918 above. No evidence status change.
requirements.ad_fd_test_note: AD-vs-FD consistency on the normalize='flux' S-matrix path (issue #148); CPU, unmarked.

requirements.recommended_reference: openEMS/HFSS coaxial matched/short/open/load reference at a calibrated TEM reference plane
missing_evidence: evidence artifacts live in gitignored .omx/, not committed; clean-checkout audit blocked
requirements.notes: M21 is gap replay only. M35 adds a narrow rfx/openEMS coaxial-gap magnitude comparison, but it is not calibrated TEM reference-plane evidence and does not promote add_coaxial_port(...) S-parameters. M61 adds a synthetic TEM reference-plane V/I extraction oracle with max_s11_abs_diff 1.04e-8, reducing the calibration-helper layer. M62 adds a synthetic Cartesian field-plane adapter oracle with max_s11_abs_diff 8.29e-6, reducing the DFT-plane adapter layer. M63 captures real FDTD DFT planes and routes them through the M61/M62 replay path, but it is blocked by near-zero transverse TEM V/I at the sampled plane and plane/gap S11 max_abs_diff 4.996. M64 sweeps 11 z-planes from pin tip to gap and finds 0 passed planes; best plane/gap S11 max_abs_diff is 0.628 and best signal score is 1.25e-16, so the blocker is not just a single-plane selection issue. M65 identifies structural causes: axial/normal source component and no PEC annular outer-shell cells in the sampled default stamp. M66 fixes the annular outer-shell stamp (12/32 shell-band cells now PEC), but the axial source blocker remains and the post-fix sweep still has 0/11 passing planes with best plane/gap S11 max_abs_diff 0.996. M67 demonstrates a distributed transverse E/M plane-source prototype can produce nonzero TEM-plane V/I with max |S11| 0.168, but it is not the public add_coaxial_port API, not an external full-wave reference, and not a broad E5 envelope. M72 promoted the M67 plane-source prototype into the public Simulation.compute_coaxial_s_matrix(...) API and the 9-test tests/test_coaxial_s_matrix.py suite (schema, family-routing rejection, Z_TEM identity, amplitude-scale invariance); per-frequency |S11| was not yet calibrated against analytic PEC-cavity reflection. M73 unblocks the calibration: (a) wires the missing setup_coaxial_port call into compute_coaxial_s_matrix so the PEC pin / PTFE / PEC outer shell are actually stamped (the previous M72 ran the source in pure vacuum), (b) restructures build_coaxial_tem_plane_source_specs as a proper TFSF equivalent-currents pair (Yee-staggered e_inc/h_inc with Δt = dt/2 + dz·sqrt(εr)/(2c), spatial half-cell offset between E and H source planes, cross-coupling between H sources and the E mode profile, local Z_med = sqrt(μ/ε) for the h_inc normalisation, εr in coeff_e for the dielectric cb, source-cell radial filter [pin_radius, shell_inner] so cells don't fall inside the M66 PEC shell), (c) adds the port-direction-aware current_sign in extract_coaxial_plane_vi_from_dft so the V/I extractor convention matches the source's forward direction. The xfail strict marker on the PEC-short calibration test is now removed and the test passes at the bumped fixture freq_max=20 GHz (gives ~1 PTFE cell of resolution; the original 10 GHz had 0.47 cells of PTFE which cannot support a discrete coax TEM mode). M74 (2026-06-08) COMPLETES broad-E5 + broad-E4 -> current_status=broad_e5_passed. (a) broad-E5 ENVELOPE: scripts/diagnostics/coax_line_broad_e5_envelope.py runs the promoted Simulation.compute_coaxial_line_reflection vs EXACT analytic transmission-line truth over short/open/matched + resistive 25/100ohm, two characteristic impedances (48.6/63 ohm) and a mesh axis (annulus 1.9->5.7 cells); method cases (short/open/resistive) max |Gamma| dev 0.037 <= 0.05, evidence_level E5-broad-... (artifact 2026-06-07-coaxial-line-broad-e5-envelope). (b) broad-E4 external full-wave: MEEP power-flux (LINEAGE A conda-forge pymeep) cross-validates short/open (|Gamma|=1) vs rfx over 4-12 GHz, max |S11| diff 0.063 (driven by the 4 GHz band edge where the Gaussian source SNR is ~5 orders below peak; mid-band 5-11 GHz <= 0.035), evidence_level E4-broad-... (scripts/diagnostics/meep_coax_line_reference.py producer + build_coaxial_line_meep_broad_comparison.py consumer, artifact 2026-06-08-coaxial-line-meep-broad). matched (|Gamma|=0, 0-by-construction in a PML-terminated line) and the resistive 25/100ohm loads stay analytic-E5-only (a MEEP 1-cell resistive sheet merely re-tests rfx's own resistor stamp and is staircase-sensitive -> R5 trap). DO-NOT-REPEAT: openEMS radial AddLumpedPort cannot excite a coax TEM on cluster openEMS v0.0.35 (no AddCoaxialPort); 3 cluster runs failed (mesh-misalign->fixed, MUR-on-PTFE instability->PML_8, then weak/zero coupling) before pivoting to MEEP. The superseded build_coaxial_line_openems_broad_comparison.py retains the stability(PML_8)+Z0(b=2.055)+sealed-shell fixes for the record.
requirements.ad_fd_test_note: extractor is numpy / not-traceable (test_ad_surface_contract::test_coaxial_reflection_extraction_breaks_tape proves it breaks the tape); no AD-vs-FD test BY DESIGN -> the differentiable moat is unavailable and MUST be resolved (e.g. a differentiable reflection path) before broad_e5_passed.

requirements.ad_fd_test_note: planned only; no AD path yet.

next_validation_target_decision: 2026-06-11 (W5.6 backlog decision, recorded NOT started): next port family for external validation = lumped_port (most existing scaffolding: narrow openEMS PEC-box + position-sweep comparisons already in evidence; needs the broad matched/open/short/load external fixture set). wire_port second (blocked_absolute_external_reference, needs an absolute external reference design first). Both are analytic-E5-only effort class.
